---
title: Copilot / Agent Workspace Instructions
---

## Purpose

This file gives short, actionable guidance for Chat/IDE agents working in this repository. Keep it brief — link to docs for deep dives.

## Use When

- You want project-specific help: build, tests, architecture, or common workflows.
- You're asking for changes that affect multiple components (policy, hub, ebpf, cli).

## Key locations

- Repository root: [README.md](README.md) — general overview and install steps.
- Agent rules & onboarding: [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) — operational rules for agents and `bd` usage.
- Claude-specific helper: [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) — quick dev commands and run/test snippets.
- Developer docs: [docs/](docs) — environment and e2e testing guidance.
- Installer: [install.sh](install.sh) — installation steps used by contributors.

## Build, run and test (common commands)

- Build (release): `cargo build --release`
- Run (dev server): `cargo run -- run` or `cargo run -- start`
- Tests: `cargo test` (e2e tests in `tests/` use `cargo test --test <name>`)
- Lint & format: `cargo clippy` and `cargo fmt`

## Non-interactive shell guidance

Follow the project's non-interactive command guidance (see [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)): always prefer non-interactive flags for file operations and package managers (e.g., `cp -f`, `rm -rf`, `apt-get -y`). Avoid prompts that can hang CI or agents.

## Where to place agent customizations

- Workspace-wide instructions: `.github/copilot-instructions.md` or `AGENTS.md` (this file)
- File-level instructions: `.github/instructions/*.instructions.md` (use `applyTo` globs)
- Prompts: `.github/prompts/*.prompt.md`
- Custom agents: `.github/agents/*.agent.md`
- Skills: `.github/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`

## Tips for authors

- Keep `description` short and include trigger phrases like "build", "tests", "policy", "e2e" so the agent can discover the instruction.
- Avoid `applyTo: "**"` unless the instruction truly applies everywhere.
- Quote YAML values that include colons or special characters.

## Examples

- Quick ask: "Run the unit tests and return failing test names." — agent should run `cargo test` and report failures.
- Change scope hint: "This affects `sevorix-hub` and `sevorix-core` — update migration and tests." — agent should open those folders and suggest specific edits.

## Next steps

If you'd like, I can:

- Add example `.github/prompts/*.prompt.md` files for common tasks (run tests, produce changelog, run e2e)
- Create file-scoped instructions for `src/` and `sevorix-hub/` with `applyTo` globs

--
Generated by an agent to provide consistent guidance for workspace-aware assistants.
